name: A Quick Guide to Roasting Vegetables
recipeIngredient: Red Bell Peppers (1 pound, approx 2 medium)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 475°F.
2. For potatoes: Scrub and cut Thin Skinned Potatoes into 1/2 inch slices; mix with 2 tsp olive oil and bake in a 475°F oven for 35-45 minutes, turning occasionally until richly browned.
3. For Russet Potatoes: Peel and cut into 1 to 3 inch chunks; mix with 2 tsp olive oil and bake for 1-1.5 hours, turning occasionally until richly browned.
4. For carrots: Peel and cut into 3-inch long strips; mix with 2 tsp olive oil and bake for 20-30 minutes until tinged with brown, turning occasionally.
5. For garlic: Peel cloves; mix with 2 tsp olive oil and bake for 20-30 minutes until tinged with brown.
6. For fennel: Rinse, trim off base, bruises, stems; cut into 1 to 3 inch chunks; mix with 2 tsp olive oil and bake for about 1 hour until tinged with brown, turning occasionally.
7. For green beans: Snap ends, pull off strings, rinse; mix with 2 tsp olive oil and bake in a 475°F oven until the ends are browned, 40 minutes, turning occasionally.
8. For red onion: Peel and cut into 3/4 inch thick chunks; mix with 1/2 tsp olive oil and 2 tbsp balsamic or wine vinegar; bake for 40-50 minutes until the ends are dark brown, turning occasionally.
9. For Roma-type tomatoes: Rinse and cut in half lengthwise; rub lightly with olive oil, set cut side up in a 9x13 inch pan; sprinkle lightly with salt; bake until edges are dark brown, about 1 hour and 10 minutes.
10. For red bell peppers: Rinse and cut in half lengthwise; broil the cut side down 4-6 inches from heat for 8 minutes; let cool, then discard skins, stems, seeds; save juice; cut into strips or chunks.
11. For oriental eggplants: Trim off stems; cut in half lengthwise; turn cut side down; cut each piece into thirds; lightly rub skin with olive oil; lay skin side down in an oiled pan; bake until browned and soft when pressed, 20-30 minutes.
